title: Model Dimensionality
description: Dimensionality of a machine learning model (e.g., embedding vector size)
documentation:
## Purpose
Carries a single integer that describes the output dimensionality of a model, most commonly the size of its embedding vectors. This allows caps to advertise or negotiate the dimension of their output without needing a full model-info record.

## When to use
Use this type as a lightweight parameter or output when only the vector dimension is needed — for example, to size a vector database index or validate that an embedding model's output matches a downstream consumer's expected dimension.

## Notes
The minimum value is 1. Typical embedding models produce dimensions between 128 and 4096. The value should match the `embedding_dim` field in [LLM Model Info](https://capdag.com/browse/media:llm-model-info;json;record) or the `embedding_dim` field in [Embedding Vector](https://capdag.com/browse/media:embedding-vector;textable;record) for the same model.
